questions.question: Briefly state the two primary functions of the Control Unit (CU). (2 marks)
keyPoints: To interpret (decode) the instructions fetched from memory.
To generate the necessary timing and control signals to coordinate the operation of the ALU, registers, and other system components.

questions.question: With the aid of a diagram, describe the fetch-decode-execute cycle. Explain the role of the Program Counter (PC), Instruction Register (IR), Memory Address Register (MAR), and Memory Buffer Register (MBR). (10 marks)
keyPoints: Definition of the cycle as the fundamental process of instruction processing.
Diagram showing the three stages (Fetch, Decode, Execute) and their sequence.
Explanation of Fetch: PC provides address to MAR -> Memory read -> Instruction copied to MBR -> Instruction moved to IR -> PC incremented.
Explanation of Decode: Control Unit interprets the opcode in the IR.
Explanation of Execute: Control Unit activates components (ALU, registers) to perform the operation.

Clear description of each register's role: PC (holds next instruction address), IR (holds current instruction), MAR (holds memory address for data transfer), MBR/MDR (holds data being read from/written to memory).
